supportClaim: Source-level WinUI 3 harness readiness for cataloged public source surfaces; this is not production visual fidelity, Windows binary execution, packaged app execution, or arbitrary full WinUI compatibility.

productionOutcomeTarget.target: 126/126 catalog entries have an implemented, bounded, Windows-only, diagnostic roadmap, or explicit non-goal disposition for the harness release gate.
productionOutcomeTarget.localExecutableRequirement: Locally executable source-level entries need implementation, tests, public fixture or source fixture coverage, native WinUI reference evidence when visual, macOS artifacts, interaction and accessibility evidence where applicable, and docs.
productionOutcomeTarget.exclusionRequirement: Windows-only and non-goal entries need deterministic diagnostics or exclusions, Windows validation evidence where applicable, support-policy wording, and no misleading macOS support claim.

phaseReadinessGates.name: Component crop and reference tooling
evidence: component-evidence.json carries component crop metadata and effective per-component thresholds
visual-run.json points to the component crop directory
strict visual status fails when claimed supported or partial component crops are missing, blank, not-rendered, or above crop thresholds

phaseReadinessGates.name: Fluent token and theme foundation
evidence: skia-v2 painters use a centralized light, dark, and high-contrast token layer
token layer covers typography, fill, stroke, status colors, selected chrome, focus stroke, disabled surfaces, radius, and popup elevation
unsupported Fluent resources remain catalog diagnostics until mapped

phaseReadinessGates.name: Ring 0 Windows chrome completion
phaseReadinessGates.status: implemented for the documented source-level subset
evidence: Ring 0 strict scenarios cover shell, layout, text, commands, forms, workbench, status/progress, resources/theme, and artifacts
claimed Ring 0 supported or partial requirements require at least usable visual grade
component crop audit rejects claimed Ring 0 not-rendered rows

phaseReadinessGates.name: Ring 1 E2E visual completion
evidence: component-commands-menus-light covers static CommandBarFlyout/MenuFlyout host targets, static MenuBar chrome, and context-menu target export; strict open-popup scenarios remain source-level diagnostics until matching native popup visual references exist
collections selected and layout/media light, dark, and high-contrast scenarios remain strict

rich form input, templates, broader keyboarding, advanced collection controls, TeachingTip, MenuBar submenu behavior, and context-menu popup behavior remain planned or diagnostic until real support is implemented

phaseReadinessGates.name: All-126 catalog closure
evidence: docs/compatibility/all-catalog-readiness-audit.json accounts for all 126 entries with a per-entry production disposition, owner phase, primary blocker, evidence profile, and release gate
the audit is derived deterministically from the catalog and verified with 'winui3-mac-runner catalog-audit --check'
per-entry dispositions, blockers, and counts agree with this inventory's allCatalogReadinessAudit buckets and leave zero entries unassigned

phaseReadinessGates.name: Broader WinUI control inventory
phaseReadinessGates.status: inventory and promotion gate implemented; controls remain planned diagnostics pending evidence-gated promotion
evidence: docs/compatibility/winui-component-inventory.json broaderControlInventory enumerates 20 prioritized public WinUI controls with target family, required states, priority, disposition, and promotion exit criteria
every prioritized control is tracked as a planned not-rendered diagnostic row in entries with no false local support claim
the broader-control honesty gate fails if any control claims a rendered grade without matching catalog status, visual evidence, and interaction coverage

phaseReadinessGates.name: Materials, motion, and high-fidelity polish
phaseReadinessGates.status: approximation registry, reduced-motion and high-contrast rules, and drift dashboard implemented; surfaces remain not-rendered pending evidence-gated promotion
evidence: docs/compatibility/material-motion-approximations.json documents every materials, composition, and motion catalog surface as a deterministic approximation target or explicit exclusion, with reduced-motion, high-contrast, and provenance rules and no OS composition claim
motion surfaces are captured as deterministic end states, not animation timing
docs/visual-parity/visual-drift-dashboard.json gates component-crop drift and keeps whole-screen drift informational, with whole-screen values read from the checked-in pixel-diff artifacts

phaseReadinessGates.name: Release candidate gate
evidence: winui3-mac-runner release-candidate aggregates the deterministic local release requirements (126/126 dispositions, catalog/docs count consistency, zero unknown surfaces, broader-control honesty, no OS composition claim, gated component-crop drift, native reference provenance, release docs, and private-name scan) into artifacts/production-gates/release-candidate.json

the gate lists the external workflow requirements (full native reference capture, full strict scenario sweep, and package dry run plus release-check) so 'ready' is a release decision, not a screenshot review
README, support policy, final production gate, and production evidence view state the exact source-level support boundary

promotionRules.meaning: The entry is absent, text-only when chrome is required, catalog-only, or diagnostic-only.
promotionRules.minimumEvidence: Catalog status and deterministic diagnostic or component-evidence row.

promotionRules.meaning: The entry renders recognizable, functionally testable output for the documented subset and supports required source-level semantics; it does not imply native WinUI visual fidelity.
promotionRules.minimumEvidence: Catalog entry, public scenario, macOS artifact, component evidence, required interaction/accessibility evidence, docs, and native WinUI reference when visual.

promotionRules.meaning: The entry meets the usable bar and has reviewed component-level visual fidelity against native WinUI references across required state/theme scenarios.
promotionRules.minimumEvidence: All usable evidence plus component-region comparison, state coverage, and documented known gaps.

promotionRules.meaning: The entry has a deliberate release-gate disposition: implemented support at the required harness grade, bounded partial support with exact limits, Windows-only exclusion, diagnostic roadmap exclusion, or explicit non-goal exclusion.
promotionRules.minimumEvidence: All required implementation or exclusion evidence, support policy coverage, release dashboard coverage, and passing docs/catalog count gate.

visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: Native window chrome and full lifecycle remain outside the local macOS support claim.
Broader WinUI layout behavior remains partial; exact sizing, clipping, and scroll behavior need renderer work.
Advanced input, caret, selection, and validation visuals require later promotion.

visualReadinessFamilies.currentGrade: usable scaffold for supported command, partial flyout subset, static MenuBar chrome, and context-menu target export
visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: MenuBar submenu behavior, context-menu popup behavior, split/dropdown popup behavior, disabled item behavior, and broad keyboarding remain incomplete.
Adaptive behavior, broad keyboard routing, and richer collection templates remain partial or planned.

visualReadinessFamilies.currentGrade: usable scaffold in base, loading, and success scenarios
visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: Animation, close/action areas, and full native severity chrome need follow-up.

visualReadinessFamilies.currentGrade: usable scaffold for supported popup subset; not-rendered for planned surfaces
visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: Modal focus trapping, placement, TeachingTip, tooltip service, and full action relationships remain planned or partial.

visualReadinessFamilies.currentGrade: usable for simple resources and theme dictionaries; not-rendered or diagnostic for broad Fluent states
visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: Full Fluent dictionaries, dynamic invalidation, pointer states, and template visual states require Phase 2+ evidence.

visualReadinessFamilies.family: Materials, composition, media, and platform integration
requiredScenarios: WindowsNativeProbe smoke evidence only for harness checks
visualReadinessFamilies.currentGrade: production-ready exclusion target defined; no local macOS visual support claim
visualReadinessFamilies.nextBlocker: Mica, Acrylic, compositor effects, media, WebView2, launcher, packaged apps, and binaries remain explicit exclusions or roadmap diagnostics.

productionBlockerMapping.visualReadinessTreatment: Require native-winui reference provenance for every promoted visual scenario; synthetic probes remain smoke-only.
Keep catalog/docs count gates aligned with the JSON source of truth and keep unknowns diagnostic.
Promote only cataloged source-level surfaces with exact support boundaries and fixture evidence.
Prevent weak, poor, or not-rendered visual rows from being hidden by whole-screen passes.
Require state/theme/template coverage before promoting visual states or template-backed controls.
Treat materials and composition as deterministic approximations or exclusions until native-reference-backed.
Require interaction and accessibility artifacts for interactive or semantic visual promotions.
Use clean-room public fixtures and corpus entries only.
Track native provenance and component evidence for every promoted visual family.
Add visual gates without loosening thresholds or hiding flake.
Carry visual readiness artifacts into release evidence before publication.
Keep private-name and artifact privacy rules mandatory for every new fixture or visual artifact.
Keep support policy and dashboard wording synchronized with every promotion or exclusion.
